result:
I would give every child, everywhere, a mind that stays as wide-open and unafraid at thirty as it is at three. No automatic narrowing into tribes, no learned terror of difference, no gradual shutdown of curiosity. A single, quiet edit to human development: keep the early childhood impulse to ask “why not?” while still gaining the skills to answer it wisely.

Everything else—war, waste, hunger, loneliness—starts with a mind that has closed. Hold it open, and the fixes start inventing themselves.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
